checkpoints.description: The file trailer does not contain a Catalog dictionary.
checkpoints.plain_english: The PDF is missing its core catalog — the document is structurally broken.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Re-export the PDF from the source application. The file may be corrupted.

checkpoints.description: The Catalog dictionary does not contain a MarkInfo dictionary with Marked set to true.
checkpoints.plain_english: The PDF is not marked as a Tagged PDF, which is required for accessibility.
checkpoints.fix_hint: In Word: File → Save As → PDF → Options → check 'Document structure tags for accessibility'. In Acrobat: Tools → Accessibility → Autotag Document.

checkpoints.description: The document does not contain a page header.
checkpoints.plain_english: One or more pages are missing a proper page structure header in the PDF tag tree.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Re-export from Word or PowerPoint with accessibility tags enabled. Check that all pages are properly structured.

checkpoints.description: The document title is missing from XMP metadata.
checkpoints.plain_english: The document does not have a title set in its properties.
checkpoints.fix_hint: In Word: File → Properties → Summary tab → fill in 'Title'. In PowerPoint: File → Info → Properties → Title.

checkpoints.description: ViewerPreferences dictionary does not contain DisplayDocTitle set to true.
checkpoints.plain_english: The PDF is not configured to display the document title in the viewer window title bar.
checkpoints.fix_hint: In Acrobat: File → Properties → Initial View → Show → Document Title. Re-export from Word with full accessibility settings.

checkpoints.description: The document language is not specified.
checkpoints.plain_english: The document has no language declared. Screen readers will not know what language to use.
checkpoints.fix_hint: In Word: File → Language → Set Proofing Language → select 'English (Canada)'. In PowerPoint: Review → Language → Set Proofing Language.

checkpoints.description: Actual text or text content cannot be mapped to Unicode.
checkpoints.plain_english: Some text in the document cannot be read by assistive technology.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Avoid using special symbol fonts (Wingdings, etc.). Replace with standard Unicode characters.

checkpoints.description: Headings are not tagged with the correct heading level tags.
checkpoints.plain_english: Text that looks like a heading is not marked up as a heading in the document structure.
checkpoints.fix_hint: In Word: Apply built-in Heading styles (Heading 1, Heading 2, etc.) instead of just making text bold or large.

checkpoints.description: Heading levels are skipped (e.g. H1 followed by H3).
checkpoints.plain_english: Heading levels jump (for example, going from Heading 1 directly to Heading 3 without a Heading 2 in between).
checkpoints.fix_hint: Review all headings and ensure they follow a logical hierarchy: H1 → H2 → H3 without skipping levels.

checkpoints.description: Table header cells are not tagged as TH.
checkpoints.plain_english: The table has no column or row headers marked up — screen readers will read all cells the same way.
checkpoints.fix_hint: In Word: select the header row → Table Design → check 'Header Row'. Ensure the table has a proper first row header.

checkpoints.description: Table is missing a summary or caption.
checkpoints.plain_english: The table has no title or description to explain what it contains to someone using a screen reader.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a caption above the table. In Word: right-click table → Insert Caption.

checkpoints.description: List items are not tagged as list elements.
checkpoints.plain_english: Bullet points or numbered lists are formatted visually but not marked up as actual lists in the document structure.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Use Word's built-in list styles (Home → Bullets or Numbering) instead of manually typed dashes or numbers.

checkpoints.description: Figures are not tagged with an Alt attribute.
checkpoints.plain_english: Images and graphics in the document are missing alternative text descriptions.
checkpoints.fix_hint: In Word: right-click each image → Edit Alt Text → write a description. Mark decorative images as decorative.

checkpoints.description: An Artifact is tagged as a Figure without an Alt attribute.
checkpoints.plain_english: A decorative image is incorrectly tagged as meaningful content, but has no alt text.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Mark purely decorative images as 'Decorative' in the alt text panel — do not leave the alt text blank on tagged figures.

checkpoints.description: Colour is used as the only visual means of conveying information.
checkpoints.plain_english: Information is communicated only through colour (e.g. 'items in red are errors') without any other indicator.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Add a text label, pattern, or icon alongside any colour-coded information.

checkpoints.description: Link annotations are not properly tagged.
checkpoints.plain_english: Hyperlinks in the document are not accessible to screen readers.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Use proper hyperlink insertion (Insert → Link) rather than pasting raw URLs. Ensure link text is descriptive.

checkpoints.plain_english: Links use generic text like 'click here' or 'read more' which gives no context to screen reader users.
checkpoints.fix_hint: Replace generic link text with a description of the destination, e.g. '2024 TD Accessibility Report (PDF)'.
